name: Roasted Chickpeas
description: Crunchy roasted chickpeas, high in protein and fiber with a low glycemic impact.
whyBetter: High protein and fiber content slows glucose absorption, making it a better snack option.

name: Mixed Nuts (Unsalted)
description: A blend of almonds, walnuts, and cashews with minimal carbohydrate content.
whyBetter: Healthy fats and protein with very low carb content, resulting in minimal blood sugar impact.

name: Oat Crackers (Whole Grain)
description: Whole grain oat crackers with fiber-rich composition.
whyBetter: Whole grain oats provide sustained energy release compared to refined flour alternatives.

name: Greek Yogurt (Plain, Unsweetened)
description: High-protein, low-sugar yogurt with beneficial probiotics.
whyBetter: High protein content and low sugar make this an excellent low-glycemic snack option.

name: Dark Chocolate (70%+ Cocoa)
description: Rich dark chocolate with minimal added sugar.
whyBetter: Higher cocoa content means less sugar and more antioxidants compared to milk chocolate.

name: Multigrain Bread
description: Bread made with multiple whole grains for a lower glycemic profile.
whyBetter: Multiple whole grains provide more fiber than white bread, slowing glucose absorption.

name: Hummus
description: Chickpea-based dip rich in fiber and plant protein.
whyBetter: Chickpea base provides protein and fiber, making it a low-glycemic dip alternative.

name: Peanut Butter (No Added Sugar)
description: Natural peanut butter without added sugars or hydrogenated oils.
whyBetter: High in healthy fats and protein with very low sugar, resulting in minimal glucose impact.

name: Ragi (Finger Millet) Crackers
description: Crackers made from finger millet, rich in calcium and fiber.
whyBetter: Ragi has a lower glycemic index than refined wheat and is rich in minerals.

name: Quinoa Puffs
description: Light, crunchy puffs made from quinoa, a complete protein source.
whyBetter: Quinoa provides all essential amino acids and has a moderate glycemic index.

name: Roasted Fox Nuts (Makhana)
description: Light, crunchy roasted lotus seeds — a traditional low-calorie snack.
whyBetter: Very low in calories and carbs while being high in minerals and antioxidants.

name: Sugar-Free Digestive Biscuits
description: Whole wheat digestive biscuits made without added sugar.
whyBetter: No added sugar and whole wheat base provide a better glycemic profile than regular biscuits.

name: Lentil Chips
description: Crispy chips made from lentil flour, high in protein.
whyBetter: Lentil base provides protein and fiber, making these lower-glycemic than potato chips.

name: Overnight Oats (No Sugar Added)
description: Pre-soaked rolled oats with chia seeds and natural toppings.
whyBetter: Soaking oats increases resistant starch, and chia seeds add fiber and omega-3s.

name: Whole Grain Muesli (No Added Sugar)
description: Mix of whole grains, nuts, and seeds without added sweeteners.
whyBetter: Combination of grains, nuts, and seeds provides balanced energy with lower sugar content.

name: Edamame (Frozen)
description: Young soybeans, high in protein and fiber with minimal carbs.
whyBetter: Excellent protein-to-carb ratio with high fiber, resulting in very low glycemic impact.

name: Almond Flour Cookies (Sugar-Free)
description: Cookies made with almond flour and natural sweeteners.
whyBetter: Almond flour has virtually zero glycemic index, making these a better cookie option.

name: Whole Wheat Khakhra
description: Thin, crispy whole wheat flatbread snack from Gujarat.
whyBetter: Whole wheat base and thin preparation result in smaller portions and moderate glycemic impact.

name: Poha (Flattened Rice - Thick)
description: Thick flattened rice that can be prepared with vegetables and spices.
whyBetter: Thick variety has slightly lower GI than regular rice. Best paired with vegetables for fiber.

name: Soya Sticks
description: Crunchy sticks made from soy protein.
whyBetter: Very high in protein and low in carbs, making these an excellent low-glycemic snack.

name: Barley Porridge Mix
description: Quick-cook barley porridge, one of the lowest GI grains available.
whyBetter: Barley has one of the lowest glycemic indices among grains due to high beta-glucan fiber.

name: Brown Rice Cakes
description: Light, puffed brown rice cakes for a crunchy snack.
whyBetter: Lower glycemic index than white rice products. Best consumed with protein toppings.

name: Flaxseed Crackers
description: Crackers made primarily from ground flaxseeds.
whyBetter: Extremely high in fiber and omega-3 fats with very low net carbs.

name: Chickpea Pasta
description: Pasta made from chickpea flour, high in protein and fiber.
whyBetter: Nearly double the protein and fiber of regular pasta with a much lower glycemic impact.

name: Chia Seed Pudding
description: Pudding made with chia seeds soaked in milk or plant-based milk.
whyBetter: Chia seeds are extremely high in fiber and omega-3s with minimal net carbs.

name: Roasted Pumpkin Seeds
description: Nutrient-dense roasted pumpkin seeds, rich in magnesium and zinc.
whyBetter: Very low carb and high in minerals that may support blood sugar metabolism.

name: Coconut Chips (Unsweetened)
description: Toasted coconut chips without added sugar.
whyBetter: High in healthy medium-chain fats with low sugar content.

name: Sugar-Free Protein Bar
description: Protein bar sweetened with stevia or monk fruit, high in whey protein.
whyBetter: High protein content and sugar-free formulation keep glycemic impact low.

name: Buckwheat Noodles (Soba)
description: Japanese-style noodles made from buckwheat flour.
whyBetter: Buckwheat has a lower glycemic index than wheat and contains rutin, an antioxidant.

name: Vegetable Sticks with Guacamole
description: Fresh carrot, cucumber, and celery sticks paired with avocado-based dip.
whyBetter: Raw vegetables are very low-glycemic, and avocado provides healthy fats that slow absorption.

name: Steel-Cut Oatmeal
description: Minimally processed whole oat groats cut into pieces.
whyBetter: Steel-cut oats have a lower glycemic index than instant oats due to less processing.

name: Amaranth Puffs
description: Puffed amaranth grain, a traditional superfood snack.
whyBetter: High in protein and minerals. Best consumed in small portions due to higher GI when puffed.

name: Baked Lentil Crisps
description: Oven-baked crisps made from lentil and rice flour blend.
whyBetter: Baked instead of fried, with lentil protein reducing overall glycemic impact.

name: Parle-G Biscuits
description: Classic Indian glucose biscuit made with wheat flour and sugar.
whyBetter: Widely available, but high in sugar. Consider sugar-free digestive biscuits instead.

name: Maggi 2-Minute Noodles
description: Popular instant noodles made from refined wheat flour (maida).
whyBetter: Convenient but made from refined flour. Try whole wheat or millet-based noodles.

name: Real Activ Juice
description: Packaged fruit juice with added sugar. High glycemic impact due to fruit sugars.
whyBetter: Fruit juices lack fiber found in whole fruits. Consider whole fruits or buttermilk instead.

name: Britannia Marie Gold
description: Light tea biscuit made with wheat flour, sugar, and vegetable oil.
whyBetter: Lower fat than cream biscuits but still high sugar. Try oat biscuits for lower GI.

name: Haldiram's Aloo Bhujia
description: Traditional Indian fried snack made from potato and gram flour.
whyBetter: Gram flour adds protein but the frying and potato starch raise GI. Try roasted chana instead.

name: Amul Buttermilk (Chaas)
description: Spiced buttermilk drink, low in carbs and rich in probiotics.
whyBetter: Very low glycemic impact. Probiotics support gut health. Great alternative to fruit juices.

name: MTR Rava Idli Mix
description: Instant mix for making semolina idlis, a South Indian breakfast staple.
whyBetter: Idlis are steamed (not fried) but semolina has moderate GI. Pair with sambar for fiber.

name: Patanjali Dalia (Broken Wheat)
description: Cracked whole wheat porridge, a traditional high-fiber Indian breakfast.
whyBetter: Whole wheat retains bran and fiber, giving it a much lower GI than refined cereals.

name: Paper Boat Aam Panna
description: Traditional raw mango drink with added sugar.
whyBetter: Contains some sugar but lower than most fruit juices. Best consumed in small portions.

name: Thums Up / Cola Drinks
description: Carbonated soft drink with high sugar content (10-11g per 100ml).
whyBetter: Very high sugar. Consider sparkling water with lemon, or sugar-free alternatives.

name: Lijjat Papad
description: Thin lentil-based papad/papadum, roasted or microwaved.
whyBetter: Made from urad dal (black gram), high in protein with low carb content when roasted.

name: Aashirvaad Atta (Whole Wheat)
description: Whole wheat flour for making chapatis and rotis.
whyBetter: Whole wheat has lower GI than maida. Chapatis are a better choice than naan or white bread.

name: Saffola Oats
description: Rolled oats, can be prepared as porridge or used in various recipes.
whyBetter: Oats are rich in beta-glucan fiber which helps slow glucose absorption.

name: Bikaji Rasgulla
description: Traditional Indian sweet made from chhena (cottage cheese) in sugar syrup.
whyBetter: Very high sugar content from syrup. Consider sugar-free versions or small portions.

name: Bournvita / Malt Beverages
description: Chocolate malt drink mix, often high in sugar and maltodextrin.
whyBetter: Contains maltodextrin (GI 100) and sugar. Consider plain milk with cocoa powder instead.

name: Curd / Plain Yogurt (Homemade)
description: Plain unsweetened yogurt/dahi, a staple in Indian meals.
whyBetter: Very low GI, rich in probiotics and protein. Excellent meal accompaniment for diabetics.

name: Sprouts (Moong/Chana)
description: Sprouted green gram or chickpeas, eaten raw or lightly cooked.
whyBetter: Sprouting reduces glycemic index and increases nutrient availability. Very high protein.

name: Paneer (Cottage Cheese)
description: Fresh Indian cottage cheese, extremely low in carbs and high in protein.
whyBetter: Near-zero carbs with high protein and fat, making it an ideal low-glycemic food.

name: Ragi Dosa Mix
description: Dosa batter mix made from finger millet (ragi) flour.
whyBetter: Ragi has a lower GI than rice and is rich in calcium. Better alternative to regular dosa.

name: Sattu Drink
description: Traditional drink made from roasted gram (chana) flour, popular in Bihar and UP.
whyBetter: Very high protein, low GI. Excellent summer drink and much better than sugary beverages.

name: Dry Fruits Chikki (Sugar-Free)
description: Brittle bar made with mixed dry fruits and jaggery-free or sugar-free sweetener.
whyBetter: Nuts provide healthy fats and protein. Sugar-free versions avoid the glucose spike.
